items.title: What is Mistral AI? Everything to know about the OpenAI competitor
items.raw_content: Mistral AI, which offers some open source AI models, has raised significant funding since its creation in 2023, with the ambition to “put frontier AI in the hands of everyone.”

items.title: Anthropic wants to develop its own drugs
items.raw_content: At the event "The Briefing: AI for Science" earlier this week, Anthropic announced Claude Science, a new "AI workbench for scientists" that pulls fragmented tools and datasets into one environment, and generates figures and visuals. Anthropic, already dominating the industry with its popular coding tools and powerful AI models, framed the launch around what it […]

items.title: New Google commercial imagines a Declaration of Independence written with help from AI
items.raw_content: Two hundred and fifty years after the signing of the Declaration of Independence, a new commercial asks: What if the Founding Fathers had access to Google Workspace?

items.title: Midjourney wants Hollywood studios to reveal the details of their AI usage
items.raw_content: As part of an ongoing legal dispute with three Hollywood studios, Midjourney is seeking to compel those studios to reveal how they use AI themselves.

items.title: The fanfiction community is at war with AI — and itself
items.raw_content: Over the past week, a new fanworks movement has kicked off, with the aim to root out authors using generative AI. items.title: A behind-the-scenes look at Midjourney’s medical scanner leaves many questions unanswered
items.raw_content: Midjourney has shown more of its futuristic medical scanner. It still hasn't shown much proof it works. The AI startup, best known for generating images, released a behind-the-scenes video of its dunk-tank ultrasound scanner, which it plans to deploy in spas and hopes will transform medicine with cheap, detailed, radiation-free imaging. The nearly 20-minute tour […]

items.title: Meta quietly launches vibe-coded gaming app Pocket
items.raw_content: Meta has quietly launched Pocket, an experimental AI app that lets users generate and share interactive mini games using text prompts.
items.summary: Meta has quietly launched Pocket, an experimental AI app that lets users generate and share interactive mini games using text prompts.

items.title: Mark Zuckerberg tells staff that AI agents haven’t progressed as quickly as he’d hoped
items.raw_content: At an internal meeting, the Meta CEO reportedly said that AI development efforts were not moving as quickly as anticipated.

items.title: Anthropic’s long-sidelined Fable 5 is greenlit to return
items.raw_content: After weeks of negotiating with the Trump administration, Anthropic is finally going to be able to bring Claude Fable 5 back online. items.title: OpenAI floats giving Trump administration 5 percent cut of AI boom
items.raw_content: OpenAI has floated giving the US government a 5 percent ownership stake as a way of easing tensions with the Trump administration and blunting mounting public backlash against AI, according to the Financial Times. CEO Sam Altman argued that giving the public a financial interest in the company would be the best way to share […]

items.title: Google built a great smart speaker, but Gemini isn’t ready for it
items.raw_content: Smart speakers have spent the past few years searching for a compelling second act. Beyond music, timers, and controlling your lights, they've struggled to justify taking up space on the kitchen counter. AI promised to change that. Amazon debuted its new hardware powered by a revamped Alexa last fall, and now it's finally Google's turn. […]

items.title: Google’s NotebookLM can sum up your research in a TikTok-style clip
items.raw_content: Google's NotebookLM is adding a new way to catch up on your notes: TikTok-style AI videos.
